rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AMALIA PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Caregiving Services Of Ms. Carmen Ochoa, Wife Of Conservator Mr. Jose Ochoa
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/03/2021 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No contact information is on file. 2) No status report is on file. 3) No proof of payment of assessment is on file. 4) No amended letters have been issued to reflect the corrected name of Fred Ochoa. The name on the bond has not been corrected either. 5) Jose Ochoa and Fred Ochoa must sign the petition. CRC 7.103(a). 6) Please describe the petitioners' efforts to find independent service providers and terms available from such providers. CRC 7.1059(a)(4). 7) Both Carmen Ochoa and the conservators appear to be providing the same services (cooking, cleaning, shopping, medical appointments, etc.). How is each person's shift determined? 8) Should legal counsel be appointed to represent the conservatee's interests in this petition?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GEORGE J. DURANTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/21. Examiner needs more time to review the three related accounts as well as time sheets and fee declarations filed separately on different dates in each of the three related cases. The date (and case number if different from the case in which a disbursement was made) of any order authorizing fees must be set forth on the disbursements schedule in each account. The amount stated as having been paid for services as conservator of the person (paragraph 9) does not agree with the amount shown on the disbursements schedule in the conservatorship account (p. 17 of the account). The hours were for a period ending beyond the accounting period. Similarly, the amount of trustee fees set forth in paragraph 7 of the report for the CRT is for the period ending August 31, 2020, but the account shows entries only through April 30, 2020. The trustee of the revocable trust was authorized to pay itself $435/month but it appears to have paid itself more than that out of the revocable trust during the accounting period. Why was it necessary for the attorney to use messenger service five times in the revocable trust when the order establishing the trust and bond were filed on just two dates? The statement re retention of original statements must include language addressing the length of time the original will be retained, as set forth in LRSF 2.11 M.4.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VALENTINE FAMILY LIVING DATED MAY 13, 1994 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Expand Scope Of Guardian Ad Litem
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The pagination of the settlement agreement is incorrect; 9 of the 10 pages indicate that they are "Page 10 of 10". 2) The "parties agree to create a separate trust for the benefit of David, Jr" Court to determine whether additional language will be needed in the order to protect the interest of the minor. 3) The parties agree that the GAL will select a professional fiduciary to be appointed trustee, including over the minor's trust. Court to determine whether a petition will be needed to appoint the trustee. 4) Petitioner requests expanding the scope of her role as GAL so that she can oversee the administration of the minor's to-be created subtrust. Court to determine whether expansion can be granted when the subtrust will not be under court supervision. 5) Court to determine how to proceed on the remaining petitions. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLOS ENRIQUE DELGADILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/21. (No earlier availability.) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and the legal description of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] The Court cannot order what has not been set forth in a verified pleading. A Court-appointed fiduciary is required to sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAGDALENA T. REYES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/2021. 1) Petitioner has not filed notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120. If a citizen of a foreign country dies without leaving a will or leaves a will without naming an executor, or if it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code 8113.] 2) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/16/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY CHRISTINE OWENSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order For Preliminary Distribution From Blocked Account In 2020 To Allow Expenses To Offset The Gain On Sale Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if there is any reason to proceed with this petition now, when the purpose was ostensibly to pay certain expenses in the same year as the sale of the real property to offset capital gains. No courtesy copy of the attorney's Declaration filed on 12/23/20 has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. Two different versions of the Judicial Council form Order for Withdrawal of Funds from Blocked Account have been received: one by the assigned Examiner and one emailed to Examiner Hilton on 1/27/21 (not by 12/28/20, as directed at the hearing on 12/23/20). Costs reportedly paid by the administrator with will annexed have not yet been itemized. No calculation of the statutory compensation has been set forth. (This Court does not ordinarily permit more than 75% of the statutory compensation before final distribution. [LRSF 14.56 A.]) As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BALISTRERI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Validity Of Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KAREN GRANT 2016 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/2021. 1) Please provide legal authority for why San Francisco is the proper venue. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined before the petition will be determined. 2) Petitioner has not filed the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. 3) Please file legal authority for a trust serving as a successor trustee of the Karen Grant 2016 trust. 4) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities addressing why the property should not be deemed an asset of Karen Grant's estate. 5) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.7). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/22/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH S. BURNHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/17/21, at the direction of the judicial officer, to allow the petition to be set for hearing. attendance will be required by the petitioner and the attorney. Issue for hearing is discussion of why this petition is more appropriate and beneficial to the trust and its beneficiaries than a Petition to Determine Succession to Property, given the statutory fees required in a probate estate and the length of time of administration of an estate v. adjudication of a single petition.
